definition:
παλαιόω, -ῶ
 (παλαιός), [in LXX chiefly for בָּלָה, pi., in pass. for בִּי qal. ;] 
to make or declare old: Heb.8:13; pass., to become old: of things worn out by time and use (cf. Jhn.9:13, Isa.50:9, al., and see: παλαιός), Luk.12:33, Heb.1:11" (LXX) ; τὸ παλαιούμενον, Heb.8:13 (where this  and the act., see supr., may have the sense of abrogate, see LS, Zorell, see word).†
 SYN.: γηράσκω  (AS)
